claimText: The WHO 3 By 35 Initiative aims to raise the prices of tobacco, alcohol, and sugary drinks by at least 50% by the year 2035.
evidenceExcerpt: WHO's 3 By 35 Initiative aims to raise prices of tobacco, alcohol, and sugary drinks by at least 50% by 2035.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the target products, percentage increase, and target year of the initiative.
distractorHooks: Targeting a 35% price increase instead of 50%
Including ultra-processed foods instead of just tobacco, alcohol, and sugary drinks
Initiative launched by WTO or FAO instead of WHO

claimText: Health taxes are levied on products with negative public health impacts and are recommended by the WHO as a cost-effective tool to address obesity and non-communicable diseases (NCDs).
evidenceExcerpt: Health taxes are levied on products with negative public health impacts to address obesity and non-communicable diseases (NCDs).
examUse: Statement-based question on the definition and primary objective of health taxes.
distractorHooks: Health taxes are primarily aimed at addressing communicable diseases
Health taxes are levied on all food products to fund healthcare infrastructure
